action.hypothesis: Gated routing has now produced a clean proxy result and slightly outperformed plain top-k, so the next meaningful architecture follow-up is to test whether the family was capacity-limited by increasing expansion_factor from 8 to 16 at K=128 under the same training regime.
action.summary: Keep the gated family active and run a proxy stabilization pass at K=128 with EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 to test whether extra encoder capacity unlocks a better regime than the initial prototype.

notes_to_memory: The gated family has now run cleanly with launcher-safe overrides and slightly outperformed the plain top-k probe, so it remains the strongest non-JumpReLU architecture candidate.
This round begins gated-family stabilization by testing whether the initial prototype was capacity-limited at EXPANSION_FACTOR=8.
Using only generic overrides avoids the invalid-env failure mode seen in earlier gated attempts.
next_hypotheses: If gated with EXPANSION_FACTOR=16 improves proxy FVU materially, keep stabilizing gated and consider promotion only if the gain is strong enough.
If EF=16 is neutral or worse, conclude that generic parameter stabilization is not enough for gated and plan a code-backed family refinement instead of more blind sweeps.
If runtime degrades sharply while quality improves, keep the family alive and follow with a performance-focused round rather than discarding it.
